description: Canonical Hebrew ↔ Arabic ↔ English math/physics glossary for Cena platform. Validated against Israeli Bagrut exam terminology (2019-2024). Source of truth for all question authoring, translation, and quality gates.

notes.genderConvention: Arabic gender field: m = masculine, f = feminine. Adjectives modifying these terms must agree in gender.
notes.hebrewSource: MoE Bagrut exams 806/807/804 (2019-2024), Yoel Geva textbooks
notes.arabicSource: Israeli Arab school math/physics textbooks, Palestinian curriculum alignment (MSA)
notes.physicsNotation: SI units throughout. Hebrew physics uses Latin variable letters. Arabic physics maps Arabic letters per ArabicMathNormalizer.

terms.notes: Arabic قطر also means diameter; context-dependent
Arabic وتر also means hypotenuse; context-dependent
α — angular acceleration, thermal expansion coefficient
Δ — change in quantity. Hebrew/Arabic both use Δ in formulae directly.
ω — angular velocity. Ω — ohm (resistance unit)
π ≈ 3.14159. Symbol used directly in all three languages
σ — stress, standard deviation. Σ — summation
